seoTitle: Find and Unhide Hidden Chats in Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Recover a hidden conversation in Microsoft Teams using the search bar, then permanently restore it to your visible chat sidebar.
intro: Hiding an inactive chat is a great way to declutter your Teams sidebar, but tracking that conversation down later can be confusing if you don't know Teams keeps hidden chats rather than deleting them. This guide shows how to search for a hidden thread and permanently unhide it.

steps.heading: Open the search bar
steps.body: Click into the central search box at the top of the Teams window.

steps.heading: Search by name
steps.body: Type the name of the person or group whose conversation you're trying to find.

steps.heading: Select the matching result
steps.body: Choose the correct profile from the dropdown list to open the hidden conversation.

steps.heading: Look for the italic sidebar entry
steps.body: Once opened, the hidden thread appears in your left sidebar in italic text, distinguishing it from your active chats.

steps.heading: Unhide it permanently
steps.body: Hover over the italicized name, click the three-dot More options icon, and select Unhide to restore it to your regular chat list.

faq.question: Does hiding a chat delete the messages?
faq.answer: No, hiding a chat only removes it from your visible sidebar — the conversation and its history stay intact.

faq.question: How do I know a chat is hidden versus just old?
faq.answer: A hidden chat shows up in italic font in the sidebar and includes a 'Show hidden chat history' banner when opened.

faq.question: Is there a faster way to find hidden or buried chats?
faq.answer: Yes, the Filter funnel (Ctrl+Shift+F) under the chat column lets you sort conversations by unread or pinned status.

faq.question: Will sending a new message automatically unhide a chat?
faq.answer: Yes, replying to a hidden conversation typically brings it back into your active chat stream.
